member.name: T:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController
member.summary: API Controller for desktop application integration. Provides endpoints for stock reservations and invoice management to ensure server-side stock control and prevent negative quantities.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.CreateReservation(ShopInventory.DTOs.CreateStockReservationRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a stock reservation for a pending invoice from the desktop app. This reserves stock server-side to prevent negative quantities even before SAP posting. The reservation holds stock for a configurable duration (default 60 minutes).
member.param: The reservation request with line items
member.returns: The created reservation with allocated batches
member.remarks:
Use this endpoint when a sale is committed in the desktop app but not yet posted to SAP. The reserved stock will be held until: - The reservation is confirmed (posted to SAP) - The reservation is cancelled - The reservation expires
Sample request: ```json { "externalReferenceId": "DESKTOP-INV-2024-001", "sourceSystem": "DESKTOP_APP", "documentType": "Invoice", "cardCode": "C001", "cardName": "Customer Name", "reservationDurationMinutes": 60, "lines": [ { "lineNum": 1, "itemCode": "ITEM001", "quantity": 10, "warehouseCode": "WH01", "unitPrice": 100.00, "autoAllocateBatches": true } ] } ```

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.GetReservation(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets a reservation by its ID.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.GetReservationByReference(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets a reservation by its external reference ID.
member.param: The external reference ID from the source system

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.ListReservations(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lists reservations with filtering and pagination.
member.param: Filter by status (Pending, Confirmed, Cancelled, Expired)

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.ConfirmReservation(ShopInventory.DTOs.ConfirmReservationRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Confirms a reservation by posting it to SAP as an invoice. This completes the sale and releases the reservation.
member.remarks:
Call this endpoint when the desktop app is ready to finalize the sale. The reservation will be converted to an SAP invoice and optionally fiscalized.
Sample request: ```json { "reservationId": "abc12345-...", "numAtCard": "Customer PO #123", "comments": "Delivery note: Leave at reception", "fiscalize": true } ```

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.CancelReservation(ShopInventory.DTOs.CancelReservationRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Cancels a reservation and releases the reserved stock. Use this when a sale is abandoned or needs to be recreated.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.RenewReservation(ShopInventory.DTOs.RenewReservationRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Renews/extends a reservation before it expires. Use this to keep a reservation active if the sale is still in progress.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.GetAvailableStock(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets available stock for an item in a warehouse, accounting for existing reservations. This shows the ACTUAL available quantity that can be sold.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.GetAvailableStockBulk(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets available stock for multiple items in a warehouse. Useful for loading stock levels for a product list.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.GetAvailableBatches(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets available batches for a batch-managed item, accounting for reservations. Returns batches sorted by FEFO (First Expiry First Out).

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.ValidateStockAvailability(ShopInventory.Controllers.ValidateStockRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Validates if stock is available for a potential reservation without creating it. Use this for real-time validation as items are added to a sale.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.CreateInvoiceDirect(ShopInventory.Controllers.CreateDesktopInvoiceRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates an invoice directly without a separate reservation step. This is a convenience endpoint that reserves stock and immediately posts to SAP. Use this for quick sales where reservation is not needed.
member.remarks:
This endpoint: 1. Validates stock availability (considering existing reservations) 2. Creates a temporary reservation 3. Posts the invoice to SAP 4. Marks the reservation as confirmed
If SAP posting fails, the reservation is automatically cancelled.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.CreateQueuedInvoice(ShopInventory.Controllers.CreateDesktopInvoiceRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a queued invoice - reserves stock and queues for batch posting to SAP. This is the RECOMMENDED approach for desktop apps to avoid concurrency issues.
member.remarks:
This endpoint: 1. Validates stock availability (considering existing reservations) 2. Creates a reservation to hold the stock 3. Queues the invoice for batch posting to SAP (processed every 10 seconds) 4. Returns immediately with reservation ID and queue status
The desktop app should: 1. Call this endpoint when a sale is confirmed 2. Poll GET /queue/{externalReference} to check if SAP posting is complete 3. Display the SAP DocNum once status is "Completed"
Benefits: - No race conditions - stock is reserved immediately - No timeout issues - background service handles SAP posting - Automatic retry on transient failures - Works even if SAP is temporarily unavailable

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.GetQueueStatus(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the status of a queued invoice by external reference. Use this to poll for completion after creating a queued invoice.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.GetQueueStatusByReservation(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the status of a queued invoice by reservation ID.

member.summary: Gets all pending invoices in the queue.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.GetInvoicesRequiringReview(System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets invoices that require manual review due to repeated failures.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.CancelQueuedInvoice(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Cancels a queued invoice (only if still pending). This also releases the associated stock reservation.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.DesktopIntegrationController.CreateQueuedTransfer(ShopInventory.Services.CreateDesktopTransferRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a queued inventory transfer - queues for batch posting to SAP. This is the RECOMMENDED approach for desktop apps to handle inventory transfers.
member.remarks:
This endpoint: 1. Validates the transfer request 2. Queues the transfer for batch posting to SAP (processed every 10 seconds) 3. Returns immediately with queue status
The desktop app should: 1. Call this endpoint when a transfer is confirmed 2. Poll GET /transfer-queue/{externalReference} to check if SAP posting is complete 3. Display the SAP DocNum once status is "Completed"

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.InvoiceController.CreateInvoice(ShopInventory.Models.CreateInvoiceRequest,System.Boolean,ShopInventory.DTOs.BatchAllocationStrategy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a new invoice in SAP Business One with comprehensive batch validation. For batch-managed items: - Validates batch allocations at the batch level (not just warehouse) - Auto-allocates batches using FIFO/FEFO if not explicitly specified - Prevents negative quantities at both batch and warehouse level - Uses short-lived locks to prevent race conditions
member.param: Whether to auto-allocate batches using FIFO/FEFO (default: true)
Batch allocation strategy: FEFO (default) or FIFO
member.returns: The created invoice with batch allocation details

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.InvoiceController.GetAvailableBatches(System.String,System.String,ShopInventory.DTOs.BatchAllocationStrategy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets available batches for an item in a warehouse, sorted by FIFO/FEFO strategy. Use this endpoint to see which batches are available before creating an invoice.
member.returns: List of available batches sorted by the specified strategy

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.InvoiceController.ValidateInvoice(ShopInventory.Models.CreateInvoiceRequest,System.Boolean,ShopInventory.DTOs.BatchAllocationStrategy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Validates an invoice request without creating it. Use this endpoint to check if an invoice would be valid before submitting.
member.returns: Validation result with allocated batches (if successful)

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Controllers.ReportController.CreateReportTimeout
member.summary: Creates a cancellation token that is NOT tied to the client disconnection. Reports are cached (900s), so we want them to complete even if the client disconnects — the next client request will get the cached result. Uses a 3-minute timeout to prevent SAP queries from running indefinitely.

member.name: T:ShopInventory.Middleware.IdempotencyMiddleware
member.summary:
Middleware to prevent duplicate form/API submissions using idempotency keys. Protects against: Double-submit attacks, duplicate invoice posting, race conditions.
Usage: Clients send an "Idempotency-Key" header with POST/PUT/PATCH requests. If the same key is seen within the expiration window, the request is rejected.

member.name: T:ShopInventory.Middleware.SAPConcurrencyHandler
member.summary: DelegatingHandler that limits the number of concurrent HTTP requests to the SAP Service Layer. Prevents parallel operations (Task.WhenAll in validation/reports) from overwhelming SAP with too many simultaneous requests on a single session.

member.name: T:ShopInventory.Middleware.SecurityHeadersMiddleware
member.summary: Middleware to add security headers to all responses. Protects against: Clickjacking, XSS, MIME sniffing, data leakage, content injection.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Middleware.SecurityHeadersMiddleware.IsSensitivePath(System.String)
member.summary: Paths that contain sensitive data and must never be cached by browsers.

member.name: T:ShopInventory.Middleware.RequestValidationMiddleware
member.summary: Middleware to validate and block suspicious/malicious requests. Protects against: SQL Injection, XSS, Path Traversal, Command Injection, XXE, Open Redirects. Scans URL path, query string, headers, and request body.

member.name: T:ShopInventory.Middleware.FileUploadValidationMiddleware
member.summary: Middleware to enforce file upload security policies. Protects against: Malicious file uploads, oversized files, path traversal via filenames.

member.name: T:ShopInventory.Middleware.RequestSizeLimitMiddleware
member.summary: Middleware to enforce request size limits and prevent payload-based DoS.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Services.SAPServiceLayerClient.SafeSapIdentifierRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^[a-zA-Z0-9\\-_\\./\\s]+$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a character in the set [\--9A-Z_a-z\s] greedily at least once.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: M:ShopInventory.Services.SAPServiceLayerClient.IsBusinessPartnerDataError(System.String)
member.summary: Detects SAP errors related to corrupted or invalid Business Partner data. Error -2028: Referenced record not found (e.g., deleted Discount Group in ODIS table). Error -1025: Corrupted BP record — object found but cannot be loaded due to broken linked data in related tables (CRD1 addresses, OCRB bank accounts, OCPR contacts, ODIS discount groups, OCTG payment terms).
